lines.en_lit: They said to Rav Yosef: This is what Rava does. said to him: May it be will that you lift your head over the entire city,

Rabbi Alexandri said Rabbi Yehoshua ben Levi said: One who prays must take three steps backward and then recite: Peace, Rav Mordekhai said to him: Since he has taken three steps backward, he should stand there analogous to a student who takes leave of his teacher. If he returns immediately he is similar to a dog who returns to its vomit,

this was also taught One who prays must take three steps backward and then recite: Peace. And if he did not do so, it is better for him not to have prayed, And they said in the name of Shemaya that one recites: Peace, to the right and then to the left, as it is stated: “At His right hand was a fiery law to them” and it says: “A thousand may fall at your side, and ten thousand at your right side”
What is And it says? If you say it is the usual manner to give with the right come hear: “A thousand may fall at your side, and ten thousand at your right side,”

Rava saw Abaye reciting: Peace, to right first. said to him: Do you maintain to your right? I say to your left as it is the right of the Holy One, Blessed be He, Rav Ḥiyya, son of Rav Huna, said: I observed that Abaye and Rava took three steps all in one bowing,

And recites a brief prayer in the outer chamber. What he pray? Rava bar Rav Adda and Ravin bar Rav Adda both said in the name of Rav May it be your will, Lord our God, that this year shall be rainy and hot. heat a good Rather, say If hot, may it be rainy,

Rav Aḥa, son of Rava, concluded in the name of Rav Yehuda: May the rule of power not depart from the house of Judah; and may your nation Israel not depend on each other for sustenance, and let not the prayer of travelers enter Your presence

Rabbi Ḥanina ben Dosa was walking on the road rain fell upon him. He said: Master of the Universe, the entire world is comfortable and Ḥanina is suffering. The rain stopped. When he came to his house he said: Master of the Universe, the entire world is suffering and Ḥanina is comfortable. The rain came Rav Yosef said: What effect does the prayer of the High Priest have with regard to Rabbi Ḥanina ben Dosa?

The Sages taught an incident a certain High Priest who extended his prayer, and his fellow priests counted, to go in after him They began to enter and he emerged. They said to him: Why did you extend your prayer? He said to them: Does it trouble you that I prayed for you and for the Temple not to be destroyed? They said to him: Do not make a habit of doing so, as we learned: He would not extend his prayer, so as not to alarm the Jewish people,

MISHNA: After the Ark was taken there was a rock from the days of the early prophets, and was called foundation three fingerbreadths higher than the ground, and would place on it. he took the blood from who was stirring it, He entered into the place that he entered, and stood at the place where he stood And he sprinkled from one upward and seven downward.

And he would neither intend to sprinkle upward nor downward, but rather like one who whips, And this he would count One; one and one; one and two; one and three; one and four; one and five; one and six; one and seven. emerged and placed on the golden pedestal in the Sanctuary.

They brought him the goat He slaughtered it and received its blood in the bowl. He entered into the place that he entered, and stood at the place that he stood, and sprinkled from one upward and seven downward. And this he would count, One; one and one; one and two; etc. emerged and placed on the second golden pedestal in the Sanctuary. Rabbi Yehuda says: There was only one pedestal there, took the blood of the bull and placed the blood of the goat

And he sprinkled from on the curtain opposite the Ark from outside one upward and seven downward, and he would not intend, etc. And this he would count, etc. he took the blood of the goat and placed blood of the bull And he sprinkled from on the curtain opposite the Ark from outside, one upward and seven downward, etc. he poured the blood of the bull into the blood of the goat and placed from the full empty

GEMARA: not teach: After was buried, but: After was taken. we learned in accordance with the one who said the Ark was exiled to Babylonia as it was taught Rabbi Eliezer says: The Ark was exiled to Babylonia, as it is stated: “And at the turn of the year King Nebuchadnezzar sent and brought him to Babylonia, along with the precious vessels of the House of the Lord”
Rabbi Shimon ben Yoḥai says: The Ark was exiled to Babylonia, as it is stated: nothing [davar] shall be left, says the Lord” These are the Ten Commandments [dibrot] that are inside it;

Rabbi Yehuda ben Lakish says: The Ark was buried in its place, as it is stated: “And the ends of the staves were seen from the sacred place before the partition, but they could not be seen without; and they are there to this day”

this disagrees with of Ulla, as Ulla said: Rabbi Matya ben Ḥarash asked Rabbi Shimon ben Yoḥai in Rome: And since Rabbi Eliezer taught us once and twice, the Ark was exiled to Babylonia. The first that which we said: “And brought him to Babylonia, along with the precious vessels of the House of the Lord.” What is the second As it is written: “And gone from the daughter of Zion is
